data.title: Allbirds, the trendy sneaker brand that swept Silicon Valley, has also gone all-in on AI.
data.description:
Author: seed.eth, BitpushNews

Leonardo DiCaprio invested, Obama and Cook competed to wear it, Emma Watson actively endorsed it…

However, this "top middle-class brand" is not selling well.

On April 15, 2026, Allbirds, a popular American sneaker brand, announced a major development: the company would completely abandon its shoe manufacturing business and fully shift its focus to artificial intelligence computing infrastructure, renaming itself "NewBird AI". Following the announcement, the stock price surged from less than $3 to a high of over $24 during the day, a single-day increase of over 800%. Just two weeks ago, this once-star brand sold all its intellectual property and footwear assets for a paltry $39 million—a mere one percent of its peak market capitalization of $4.1 billion. From wool shoes worn by Silicon Valley elites to a shell company turning to tinkering with GPU computing power—Allbirds' story is more than just the rise and fall of a startup; it reveals the madness of today's capital markets: AI is a panacea. The Rise and Fall of the "Silicon Valley Miracle Shoes" In 2015, former New Zealand professional football player Tim Brown and renewable resources expert Joey Zwillinger founded Allbirds in San Francisco. Their vision was simple and clear: to create a comfortable shoe that didn't rely on petroleum-based raw materials, using natural materials such as merino wool and eucalyptus fiber. In 2016, their first product, the Wool Runner, was launched and quickly became a hit in Silicon Valley's tech circles. Google co-founder Larry Page, Apple CEO Tim Cook, and even former US President Barack Obama became fans of these wool shoes. Allbirds caught two excellent trends. First, the golden age of the DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) model—bypassing traditional retail channels, reaching consumers directly through the official website, and gaining complete user data and brand narrative control. Second, the ethical awakening of "sustainable consumption"—with environmental protection becoming a global consensus, a pair of shoes with a "zero carbon footprint" is itself a declaration of values. When these two narratives took root in the fertile ground of an economic upswing, Allbirds quickly grew from a Kickstarter crowdfunding project into a publicly traded company valued at over $4 billion. However, Allbirds' fall was almost as swift as its rise. Its business collapse followed a typical DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) script: a single blockbuster product supported the entire brand, and before its foundation was solid, it rushed into apparel and brick-and-mortar retail, resulting in an overextended strategy and a loss of brand focus. As more and more brands began to talk about environmental protection, and as competitors like Hoka and On surpassed it in performance and design, Allbirds' sustainability narrative was rapidly diluted. In 2022, the company's revenue reached a historical peak of $298 million; however, it subsequently declined steadily, falling to $152 million by 2025, nearly halved. Over the past five years, despite accumulating approximately $1.2 billion in sales, total losses reached a staggering $419 million. In 2024, the company received a delisting warning from Nasdaq due to its stock price remaining below $1 for 30 consecutive days, subsequently managing to maintain its listing status through a reverse stock split. In February 2026, Allbirds announced the closure of all its full-price retail stores in the United States. On March 30, 2026, Allbirds signed an agreement with brand management company American Exchange Group to sell its intellectual property and related assets for $39 million. The buyer owns brands such as Aerosoles and Ed Hardy and will continue to sell footwear products under the Allbirds name. The price has also been reduced. I checked the prices on the website today, and shoes that used to cost over $100 are now discounted to around $30... The listed company's "shell" awaits its next fate—and the answer came faster than anyone expected. From selling shoes to buying GPUs, the announcement on April 15th can be described as one of the most unexpected transformations in business history. Allbirds announced that it had reached a convertible bond financing agreement of up to $50 million with an institutional investor. The funds will be used to acquire high-performance GPU hardware and provide computing power access services to customers through a long-term leasing model. The company plans to change its name to "NewBird AI," with a long-term vision of becoming a "fully integrated GPU-as-a-Service (GPUaaS) and AI-native cloud solutions provider." The market reaction was almost frenzied. At the close of trading on April 14th, Allbirds' market capitalization was only about $21 million; after the announcement, the stock price briefly touched $24.31, and the market capitalization ballooned to approximately $165 million. On Fidelity Investments' trading activity list, Allbirds became one of the most actively traded stocks that day, demonstrating the enthusiasm of retail traders. This frenzied market reaction was less about pricing in NewBird AI's fundamentals and more about pricing in the "AI" label itself. Allbirds' transformation announcement revealed no specific information about customer resources, the technology team, or data center deployment plans—aside from $50 million in cash and a vague blueprint of "buying GPUs and then leasing them out." Independent consultant Bruce Winder commented, "I don't think Allbirds can bring anything substantial, except for brand awareness." It's worth noting that while shifting towards AI, Allbirds is also requesting shareholder approval in its SEC filings to amend its articles of incorporation to remove the phrase "serving the public interest"—meaning that this company, once proud of its B Corp certification, is actively shedding its environmental mission. From "saving the planet" to "selling computing power," Allbirds' value leap may be more symbolic than the business transformation itself. The AI ​​narrative remains the most powerful business magic. Allbirds is not the first, and certainly won't be the last. Over the past 18 months, from fast fashion to fresh food e-commerce, from logistics companies to home furnishing brands, a large number of traditional enterprises have rushed to label themselves as "AI." The reason is actually quite simple: the price-to-earnings ratio of shoe sellers is only slightly over 10, while that of computing power sellers can be inflated to over 50; GPUs are now a hard currency, more sought after than gold, and whoever has priority in delivery holds the key to resale profits; in addition, consumers' wallets are indeed thinner, so instead of continuing to spend money on advertising and competing with Temu for traffic, it's better to step back and gamble on enterprise-level AI computing power leasing—at least the story sounds better. Looking at history in a broader context, this kind of "changing clothes" is not new. During the crypto boom of 2017, a beverage company, Long Island Iced Tea, changed its name to "Long Blockchain Corp.", and its stock price surged by nearly 300% in one day, only to be delisted from Nasdaq the following year. In 2024, many Bitcoin mining companies shifted their focus to AI data centers, with Core Scientific being one of the most successful examples. From the dot-com bubble to blockchain, and now to AI, the capital market's script has never changed: the market is priced before profitability, and the narrative precedes reality. Allbirds' transformation essentially involved exchanging its remaining brand reputation and listed company status for a GPU procurement contract. The core issue is whether this entry ticket is truly valuable. AI infrastructure is a highly capital-intensive industry with extremely high technological barriers. The GPU leasing market already has players valued at billions of dollars, as well as deep involvement from massive cloud service providers like Amazon AWS and Microsoft Azure. Whether a company that once made shoes can survive in this crowded market with only $50 million in funding and a single GPU setup remains a huge unknown. Not to mention, this funding still needs approval at the special shareholder meeting on May 18th. Bloomberg industry analyst Poonam Goyal commented, "This move allows it to exit a structurally low-margin footwear and apparel business and enter a higher-value computing power business, but the execution risk remains high." We are witnessing a footnote to an era: any entity—whatever it was before—can be redefined as an AI company. As long as the story is compelling enough, capital will buy it. The AI ​​narrative remains the most powerful business magic today.

data.title: Will quantum computing soon crack Bitcoin's private keys?
data.description:
Author: Nian Nian's Friend, Cryptocurrency Research Expert

In March 2026, Google's quantum AI team, in collaboration with the Ethereum Foundation and Stanford University researchers, released a white paper detailing optimizations specifically for the secp256k1 elliptic curve used by Bitcoin and Ethereum. This reduced the required logical qubits from thousands to less than 1200, while another optimized path required approximately 1450 logical qubits. The quantum circuit built by the team could reverse engineer Bitcoin's private key in about 9 minutes. This news sent shockwaves through the cryptocurrency community.

Some self-media articles, such as "Shor's Algorithm Achieves a Historic Breakthrough: The Threshold for Quantum Computing to Crack Cryptographic Keys Drops Sharply," compiled related research and news reports, suggesting that quantum computing would soon crack Bitcoin's password, attracting widespread attention. However, this is not the case.

This article attempts to analyze the following: I. There is no risk that Bitcoin private keys will be cracked by quantum computers in the next two to three years. Considering the current state of technology in quantum computing, cryptography, and blockchain, the risk of Bitcoin private keys being cracked by quantum computers in the next two to three years is extremely low, essentially zero. While the online article is largely accurate in citing real research, it suffers from serious misinterpretations of the research conclusions and optimistic extrapolations regarding engineering implementation. Currently, all quantum computers (including the most advanced systems) are at least an order of magnitude larger than the fault-tolerant quantum computers required to crack elliptic curve cryptography, and several engineering hurdles remain to be overcome.

II. Verification of the Authenticity of Core Papers

The four core studies cited in the online article have all been verified to be authentic, as detailed in the table below:

All of the above studies were published between 2025 and 2026. Among them, the CRYPTO 2025 paper has undergone peer review and is a paper from a top international cryptography conference, so its authority is beyond doubt.

III. The Most Serious Conceptual Misunderstanding: Logical Quantum Bits ≠ Physical Quantum Bits

The most serious misleading aspect of the widely circulated article lies in its deliberate confusion between the concepts of logical quantum bits and physical quantum bits.

  • Logical Quantum Bits: "Virtual" quantum bits after quantum error correction encoding, are the actual computational units required by Shor's algorithm, and do not inherently contain physical noise.

  • Physical Quantum Bits: The actual physical units in quantum computer hardware, susceptible to environmental noise interference, requiring a large amount of redundancy to construct a single logical quantum bit.

The 1200 logical quantum bits proposed in the Google white paper, after error correction encoding, require approximately 500,000 physical quantum bits.

The 10,000 physical qubits proposed by the Oratomic team have a clock speed two to three orders of magnitude slower than the superconducting architecture, making it approximately 10 days to crack one ECC-256, thus failing to achieve the "9-minute transaction hijacking." An online article, by "multiplying and superimposing" parameters from different architectures, time windows, and preconditions, concludes that "tens of thousands of physical qubits are sufficient to crack the code"—in the parlance of StartBitcoin.org's analysis, this is "Frankensteinian mathematics," completely untenable in engineering reality.

IV. Current Technological Status: The Gap Remains Huge

Currently, the most advanced quantum processors only have a few hundred to a few thousand physical qubits, and their error rate is far higher than the fault tolerance level required to run Shor's algorithm. Currently, fault-tolerant neutral atom systems have only about 500 qubits, which is about 20 times less than the 10,000 qubits required in the paper.

... Furthermore, a key flaw in the "Chevignard scheme" mentioned in the online article has been deliberately omitted: while reducing the number of logical qubits, the scheme increases the number of Toffoli gate operations by more than 1000 times. This is equivalent to trading time for space, greatly increasing the requirements for the stability of the quantum computer. V. Core Logical Errors in the Online Article 1. Conceptual Confusion: Deliberately confusing "logical quantum bits" with "physical quantum bits," using the former's number to create panic. 2. Parameter Multiplication and Superposition: Multiplying the number of logical bits in the superconducting architecture with the error correction overhead of the neutral atom architecture to arrive at the erroneous conclusion of "tens of thousands of physical bits." 3. Selective Ignoring of Running Time: Although the neutral atom architecture requires fewer physical bits, it takes up to 10 days to crack, making it impossible to achieve "9-minute transaction hijacking." 4. Confusing Existence with Feasibility: The existence of a theoretical research result does not equate to engineering feasibility; the existence of an algorithm does not mean the hardware is ready. VI. Correctly Understanding the Boundary Conditions of "9 Minutes" The number "9 minutes to crack Bitcoin" is theoretically and physically valid, but its meaning is far narrower than suggested in online articles: These 9 minutes represent the time it takes for a quantum computer to complete the final calculation using only the exposed public key after all pre-calculations unrelated to the public key have been completed. This time is based on the assumption of microsecond-level gate operation speed and applies only to superconducting architectures. The time required to break the neutral atom architecture is measured in days. To achieve this theoretical capability, a fault-tolerant quantum computer with approximately 500,000 physical qubits needs to be built—currently, the most advanced superconducting quantum processors have only about 1,000 physical qubits. The key point is that the "9 minutes" is a description of a single operation assuming the machine already exists, not a prediction of when the machine can be built. The leap from 1,000 to 500,000 qubits represents a 500-fold increase in scale, not a gradual upgrade.

VII. Industry Authoritative Consensus: The Quantum Threat is a "Medium- to Long-Term System Upgrade Cycle," Not "Doomsday Within Two or Three Years"

There is a significant difference between the judgments of authoritative industry institutions and the doomsday narratives circulating online:

  • Bernstein (Wall Street Brokerage):: Quantum risk should be viewed as a "medium- to long-term system upgrade cycle," and the cryptocurrency industry has a three- to five-year window for post-quantum cryptography migration

  • Grayscale (Digital Asset Management Company):: Quantum risk is "real but controllable" for Bitcoin; technical solutions already exist; the real obstacle is community governance consensus

  • IX. Summary

    Core Judgment: Theoretical breakthroughs in quantum computing are progressing at an unexpectedly rapid pace, and this trend is real. However, "accelerated theoretical breakthroughs" do not equate to "attack tools being ready." From 1,000 physical qubits to 500,000, from laboratory demonstrations to large-scale fault-tolerant operation, quantum computing still faces fundamental challenges in three dimensions: physics, engineering, and cost. The real challenge facing the Bitcoin community is how to complete the system-level migration to quantum-resistant cryptography over a longer timeframe (approximately 5-10 years), rather than dealing with a non-existent quantum apocalypse within two or three years.

data.title: The Trump family has begun cutting ties with WLFI; it seems they're preparing to take over?
data.description:
In the early hours of the morning, Justin Sun once again attacked World Liberty Financial (hereinafter referred to as WLFI), accusing WLFI, a project endorsed by the entire Trump family, of openly engaging in fraud!

Moreover, while Justin Sun was taking legal action, many sharp-eyed netizens also noticed that the Trump family, who were once the founders of WLFI, have now quietly disappeared.

(Earliest data shows Trump and Witkoff as project founders)

The lending incident triggered a scandal

This round of WLFI scandals started with a lending incident. WLFI staked 5 billion of its own governance tokens on a DeFi lending platform called Dolomite and lent them out.

$75 million in stablecoins. This operation pushed Dolomite's USD1 lending pool utilization to 93%, with almost all USD1 deposited by other users being borrowed by WLFI, making normal withdrawals impossible. The token fell nearly 15% that day, hitting a new all-time low, currently priced at around $0.079, a drop of over 82% from its all-time high of $0.46 last September.

Subsequently, someone claiming to be a Web3 ambassador for World Liberty Financial revealed that 600,000 wallets bought $WLFI coins, resulting in a loss of $3.87 billion, implying that the money went into the pockets of the Trump family.

Then, someone claiming to be a Web3 ambassador for World Liberty Financial revealed that 600,000 wallets bought $WLFI coins, losing $3.87 billion, implying that the money went into the pockets of the Trump family.

Token unlocking governance proposal sparks controversy

Today, the WDFI project team proposed a governance proposal regarding token unlocking:

  • Advisors/Institutions/Founders/Team: 2-year full lock + 3-year linear release, but 10% of the tokens (approximately 4.52 billion) must be forcibly burned first.

  • Early supporters: 2-year full lock + 2-year linear release, continuous participation in governance is required during the lock-up period.

  • Furthermore, a particularly assertive point is: If the new proposal is not accepted, the tokens will be permanently frozen.

    This is the proposal that Sun Yuchen previously accused, the main contents of which are:

    1. Oppose and be punished—to vote against it, tokens will be permanently frozen. This is not voting, it is coercion

    2. Selective freezing of voting rights—He himself holds about 4% of the voting rights, but his tokens are frozen, preventing him from participating. The outcome was predetermined before the vote.

    3. Anonymous multi-signature actually controls the power—The real control of the contract comes from 3/5 anonymous multi-signature wallets. All on-chain governance votes are just a performance.

    4. Anonymous rulers, voters must be real-name—Holders must be identified to vote, but those who hold the power of life and death do not show their faces.

    5. Billions of assets illegally disposed of—Under the above conditions, forcibly deciding on token destruction and ownership redistribution is a systemic violation of property rights.

    The Trump family begins to cut ties.

data.title: Warsh at the helm of the Federal Reserve: A crypto "insider" within a hawkish monetary framework.
data.description:
Shaw.AI, Jinse Finance

The U.S. Senate Banking Committee has announced that the nomination hearing for Kevin Warsh, the former Federal Reserve Governor and nominee for Chairman of the Federal Reserve, will be held on April 21, 2026. As a key candidate strongly promoted by the Trump administration, Warsh's financial disclosures and public statements have released key signals: he is not only a monetarist hawk, but also an "insider investor" deeply involved in the crypto and fintech sectors. Of his over $100 million in assets, crypto-related investments are deeply intertwined with Wall Street family offices, and his policy stance exhibits a complex characteristic of "prioritizing balance sheet reduction, supplementing with interest rate cuts, strictly regulating private blockchains, and promoting official blockchains." This article explores this from three dimensions: crypto asset correlation, monetary policy inclinations, and potential market impact, and answers core questions in the form of FAQs.

I. Deep Integration with Crypto Assets: An Intertwined Interest Between Investors and Policymakers Warsh's latest financial disclosures reveal that his personal assets far exceed $100 million. His investments in crypto and fintech span the entire chain, from infrastructure and application platforms to prediction markets, and are highly correlated with top Wall Street capital. 1. Crypto and Web3 Portfolio Overview (Disclosed) Tenderly: An Ethereum developer platform providing on-chain data, smart contract debugging and monitoring tools, and is a core infrastructure for DeFi and NFT development. Electric Capital Related Investments: Acting as a blockchain venture capital advisor, indirectly investing in multiple early-stage crypto projects. LemonCash: A crypto financial services platform covering crypto payments, custody, and wealth management services. Stashfin: A "new consumer lending bank" integrating traditional credit with digital asset collateral. Polymarket: A prediction market platform running on crypto tokens, a native Web3 application.

  • Institutional and Fund Layer

    • Duquesne Family Office: Two investments exceeding $50 million, forming the core source of its assets. This office has a long-term focus on crypto assets.

    • Bitwise, Basis: Early-stage investments in crypto index funds and algorithmic stablecoin projects.

    • Juggernaut Fund: Over $50 million in holdings, indirectly covering the crypto and AI sectors.

    2. Crypto Policy Stance: Three Shifts from Criticism to Pragmatism

    Wash's attitude towards crypto assets has not been static, but has clearly evolved with market maturity and his own changing identity:

    • Phase One (2018-2021): Critics called cryptocurrencies "software disguised as money," defining them as The second phase (2022-2024): Pragmatists changed their tune, calling Bitcoin a "sustainable store of value similar to gold," and acknowledging its function as a "policy watchdog"—Bitcoin price fluctuations would send market signals when monetary policy decisions were flawed. They also supported wholesale central bank digital currencies (CBDCs), believing they could improve the efficiency of dollar settlements and counter the digital yuan. The third phase (2025-present): Insiders publicly stated that "Bitcoin is not a substitute for the dollar, but it is an important asset," emphasizing the financial innovation value of blockchain technology. Their personal investments and policy stances formed a dual "interest-perception" bond. II. Monetary Policy Tendencies: A "Balance Sheet Reduction for Interest Rate Cuts" Framework of Overt Dovishness and Covert Hawkishness. Warsh's monetary policy is based on monetarism, with the core argument that "inflation is a policy choice," completely different from the easing path of the Powell era, exhibiting characteristics of "short-term hawkishness, medium-term dovishness, and structural tightening." 1. Core Policy Framework: Balance Sheet Reduction Prioritized, Interest Rate Cuts Secondary. Balance Sheet (QT): Aggressive Tightening. Warsh believes the Fed's $7 trillion balance sheet is the "root cause of market distortions and inflation," advocating for accelerated balance sheet reduction after taking office ($500-800 billion per month), with a long-term goal of reducing the balance sheet to below 10% of GDP (currently around 25%). Interest Rate Policy: Conditional easing supports interest rate cuts, but only on the premise of controlling inflation through balance sheet reduction—after the core PCE falls below 2.5%, a gradual interest rate cut of 25bp will be initiated each time, forming a unique combination of "interest rate cuts + balance sheet reduction in parallel." Functional Return: Streamlining the central bank's mission requires the Federal Reserve to divest itself of non-core functions such as climate and social equity, focusing on price and financial stability, clarifying the boundaries between fiscal and monetary policies, and opposing "fiscal-led" currency issuance.

    2. Key Differences from Traditional Hawks/Doves

    III. Potential Impact on the Crypto Market and Macro Assets

    Wash's dual identity as a "hawkish liquidity provider + crypto insider" will have a structural impact on the market, with short-term tightening and medium- to long-term divergence.

    Wash's dual identity as a "hawkish liquidity provider + crypto insider" will have a structural impact on the market, with short-term tightening and medium- to long-term divergence.

    1. Crypto Market: Short-term pressure, long-term benefits for compliance and blue-chip stocks. In the short term (6-12 months), accelerated balance sheet reduction will tighten global dollar liquidity, putting pressure on overvalued altcoins and DeFi leveraged products; Bitcoin's "digital gold" positioning limits its decline. In the medium to long term (1-3 years), regulatory clarity will favor "strict regulation of private coins and promotion of official blockchains," pushing crypto into the traditional financial regulatory framework, benefiting compliant platforms and blue-chip stocks such as Bitcoin and Ethereum. Liquidity Structure Optimization: After quantitative tightening squeezes out speculative bubbles, interest rate cuts will support risk assets, with crypto and US AI stocks forming a synergy. Accelerated Institutional Entry: Warsh's background in crypto investment will reduce policy uncertainty and encourage Wall Street institutions to increase their crypto allocations. 2. Macro Assets: The US dollar strengthens initially and then stabilizes, US stocks diverge, and US Treasury volatility intensifies. US Dollar: Short-term quantitative tightening pushes up the dollar index, while medium-term interest rate cuts coupled with a slowdown in quantitative tightening lead to a weakening dollar. US Stocks: Technology (AI) and financial stocks benefit from interest rate cuts and regulatory easing, while high-debt, low-profit growth stocks are under pressure. US Treasury Bonds: Balance sheet reduction and selling of long-term Treasury bonds lead to rising long-term interest rates; interest rate cuts lower short-term interest rates, steepening the yield curve. FAQ: Warsh, the Fed, and Cryptocurrency – Core Questions Answered Q1: Does Warsh's cryptocurrency investment involve a conflict of interest? Potential conflicts of interest exist, but they comply with US government ethics rules. His disclosed cryptocurrency assets are all early investments and indirect holdings; he does not directly hold Bitcoin or Ethereum in the spot market. During the hearing, he was required to promise to "avoid voting on cryptocurrency-related policies" or "divorce sensitive assets." However, as a deep investor, his policy inclination naturally leans towards "regulatory compliance and market institutionalization," rather than comprehensive suppression. Q2: Will Warsh support Bitcoin as a legal asset or strategic reserve? He won't directly support it, but it will indirectly benefit. He explicitly stated that "Bitcoin is not a substitute for the US dollar" and opposes its inclusion in the fiat currency system; however, he acknowledges its "store of value" function and supports the development of crypto assets within a regulatory framework, forming a pragmatic complement to Trump's "Bitcoin strategic reserve" proposal. Q3: Is the combination of "balance sheet reduction + interest rate cuts" good or bad for the crypto market? Short-term negative, long-term positive. In the short term, balance sheet reduction tightens liquidity, suppressing all risky assets (including crypto); in the medium term, with inflation falling, interest rate cuts starting, and regulatory clarity, **compliant crypto blue chips (Bitcoin, Ethereum, compliant platforms)** will see valuation repair, and leverage and altcoins will continue to be cleared out. Q4: What is Warsh's stance on central bank digital currencies (CBDCs)? Support wholesale CBDCs, oppose retail CBDCs. Advocate for CBDCs to be used only for central bank-interbank settlements, improving the efficiency of cross-border dollar payments and countering the digital yuan; oppose retail CBDCs for individuals, arguing they would impact commercial bank deposits and infringe on privacy—a stance consistent with some demands from the crypto community. Q5: What are the chances of Warsh's nomination being approved? Extremely high. Senate Banking Committee Chairman Tim Scott has clearly expressed his support, and committee-level approval is expected; in the full Senate vote, Republicans unanimously support him, plus some moderate Democrats, making confirmation virtually impossible. Q6: Will the Federal Reserve adjust its crypto asset regulatory policy after Warsh takes office? It will promote "regulatory consolidation." Advocating for the inclusion of crypto assets within the existing financial regulatory framework, with coordinated regulation by the Federal Reserve, SEC, and CFTC, clarifying Bitcoin as a commodity, stablecoins as monetary equivalents, and NFTs/tokens as securities, reducing regulatory arbitrage, and benefiting compliant institutions and blue-chip assets. Conclusion: The Era of the Federal Reserve's "Crypto Insider" Warsh's emergence marks the first time the Federal Reserve has been led by an insider deeply involved in the crypto and technology sector. His policies will present a combination of "strict monetary discipline, open digital finance, and a clear regulatory framework": on the one hand, aggressively reducing the balance sheet to repair the dollar's credibility; on the other hand, embracing crypto innovation with a pragmatic attitude, driving crypto assets from "fringe speculative goods" to "mainstream compliant asset classes." For the market, short-term pain will come from tightening liquidity, while in the medium to long term, it will usher in structural opportunities with clearer regulations and institutional entry.

data.title: Dollar depreciation and declining purchasing power
data.description:
Author: Anthony J. Pompliano, Founder and CEO of Professional Capital Management

We can trace the root of many social problems back to the continuous depreciation of the US dollar over the past few years.

EJ Anthony of the Heritage Foundation recently pointed out: "Depending on the different inflation indices used, the cumulative inflation rate since January 2021 is between 23.6% and 27.1%." These are huge numbers, having a real negative impact on ordinary people. And this is only the officially released data. If we refer to the Truflation Index, its data shows that the compound inflation rate since January 2020 has approached 30%. For roughly half of Americans who hold all their assets in US dollars, this means their savings have suffered a devastating devaluation. Data from the Federal Reserve itself confirms this problem. Reports show that the M2 money supply has tripled since 2008, while the purchasing power of the dollar has declined by 38% over the same period. The savings of ordinary Americans are like ice melting under the scorching sun. But cumulative inflation and declining purchasing power don't tell the whole story. Over the past five years, nominal wages in the United States have risen considerably. There is more money circulating in the market, and some of that has indeed flowed into consumers' pockets. EJ Anthony further points out that, compared to January 2021, the purchasing power of the average American's weekly wage has decreased by 3%. Inflation has been widely reported, but wage growth has been largely overlooked. The problem is that inflation has second- and third-order effects, continuously eroding the economy and society. Housing affordability is a prime example. Home prices have been rising faster than wages, so we're watching as the home-buying ability of ordinary Americans steadily disappears. When people are worse off and left further behind, they begin to lose hope. When people lose hope, they become bolder risk-takers. Why not? What else do they have to lose? Nothing, really, isn't it? This is why we're seeing an explosive growth in sports betting, meme trading, and other high-risk investments. So what's the solution? There's a time-tested philosophy called the **Path to Success**. This path is very simple: **graduate high school, find a full-time job, and get married before having children.** If a person can do these three things, they can significantly reduce their chances of falling into economic collapse or poverty. This may seem simple to many of you, but we often overlook how many Americans end up on drastically different paths in life. I'm reminding you of this again because we are currently in the midst of a dollar devaluation crisis, while simultaneously witnessing a phenomenal bull market in commodities. According to Charlie Bilello data from Creative Planning: Sulfur: +67%; Aviation kerosene: +66%; Urea: +51%; Diesel: +50%; Heating oil: +40%; WTI crude oil: +37%; European natural gas: +34%. Gasoline: +32% Fertilizer: +31% Brent Crude Oil: +31% Coal: +14% Palm Oil: +10% Iron Ore: +7% Rice: +4% S&P 500 Index: +1% Volatility Index (VIX): -8% Commodity investors are reaping huge returns from this bull market, while ordinary Americans are suffering the negative consequences of the same price increases. The only real way to save the economy is through explosive growth led by the private sector and driven by technological innovation. We've already seen early signs of this growth in the artificial intelligence and robotics industries. We also need more sectors like aerospace, defense, and biotechnology to contribute to deflation and alleviate inflationary pressures. The US government will never stop printing money. Last month, we spent over $100 billion on interest alone on national debt—and that's just for one month. Therefore, our greatest hope lies in growth. Accelerate, accelerate, and accelerate again. It's possible. We just need to let entrepreneurs and tech talent take the lead.

data.title: A shoe company got involved in AI, and then what? Its stock price skyrocketed by nearly 600%.
data.description: A shoe company announced it's getting into AI computing power. Yesterday, its stock price surged nearly 600%. This is Allbirds, the brand known for its wool casual shoes. Overnight, it announced a "strategic transformation," aiming to enter the most crowded track: GPU computing power leasing. The story is always more interesting than the numbers. The market voted with its feet, with a sharp drop in after-hours trading. Capital is asking: How can a shoe company compete with Nvidia's customers for business? Meanwhile: PPG Industries rose over 6%, with solid performance and strong expectations. Bitcoin surged past $75,000, sending crypto stocks soaring. The Dow Jones Industrial Average dipped slightly, while the Nasdaq and S&P 500 hit new all-time highs. Who cares? At least tonight, everyone's attention is drawn to this "Cross-border Rhapsody". The core narrative of the capital market has never been "whether you did well or not", but "whether I believe your story". Allbirds told a perfect transformation story: from sluggish consumer goods to the sexiest infrastructure of the AI ​​era—computing power. Its stock price soared overnight. And then? The after-hours plunge was the first sobering slap in the face. This reveals the most authentic mentality of the current market: A thirst for narrative: still hungry for the story of "AI everything." Extreme sensitivity: zero tolerance for any logical flaws. Voting with money: buy if you believe, sell if you don't, without hesitation. What lessons can we learn from this? If you're starting a business, ask yourself: Is my business a "good story" or a "good fact"? If you're investing, be wary: Are you paying for a "vision" or for "cash flow"? Allbirds' attempt may succeed, or it may fail. That doesn't matter. Importantly, it reaffirms that ironclad rule: In the attention economy, narrative itself is an asset. But its value only exists when someone believes in it. The beginning of a story is always exciting. The ending is often a mess. Tonight, Allbirds used a stunning candlestick chart to tell the first half of the story. And the second half... I know this script all too well: The moment the news breaks, short-term funds flood in, driving the story to unprecedented heights; once things calm down, they realize, "Oh, you're still just a shoe seller," and walk away. A classic case of "the story ends and the party's over." Just enjoy it for fun; this kind of money is unattainable for ordinary people like us, and it's best not to try—it's too risky. Cryptocurrencies are celebrating, AI computing power is celebrating, and even shoe retailers are trying to jump on the bandwagon. But behind all this noise, companies quietly delivering solid financial reports continue operating at their own pace. This world needs dreamers, and it also needs bricklayers.

data.title: The US and Iran are gradually reaching a ceasefire agreement; the S&P 500 hits its first new high since the war began.
data.description:
Headline

▌US Media: US-Iran Negotiations Make Progress, Gradually Reaching a Framework Agreement to End the War

On April 15, according to AXIOS, two US officials stated that US and Iranian negotiators made progress in their talks on Tuesday, and the two sides are moving towards reaching a framework agreement to end the war. One US official stated that Trump's negotiating team (Vice President Vance, Middle East envoy Witkov, and Trump senior advisor Kushner) continues to speak with Iran and mediators by phone and exchange draft agreements, and is "close to the goal." A third official said, "We want to reach an agreement, and some people in Iran want to as well. The key is to get the entire Iranian government to agree." Sources said that if a framework agreement is reached, the ceasefire needs to be extended to negotiate details. "The details are complex and cannot be completed in two days," one official said. Another official stated that the US has not yet formally agreed to extend the ceasefire, and the two sides are still in continuous contact.

▌S&P 500 Hits First All-Time High Since Iran War

On April 16, the S&P 500 hit an all-time high during Wednesday's trading session, its first since the outbreak of the Iran war. Market expectations for a de-escalation of the conflict and strong earnings forecasts attracted investors back to risk assets. This new high amidst the ongoing geopolitical crisis signals a shift in market risk pricing, with traders appearing more willing to price in lower escalation risks in the short term. Last month, when the conflict erupted, stocks fell sharply, the oil market experienced a historic shock, and concerns about inflation and the outlook for US interest rates were reignited. Since the conflict began on February 28, the S&P 500 has fallen as much as 9%, but this did not meet the criteria for confirming a "technical correction," while the Nasdaq and Dow Jones both confirmed entering correction territory. The market was also supported by a positive corporate earnings outlook. Executives at major banks stated that despite the oil price shock, US consumers remain resilient, and there is a large backlog of mergers and acquisitions and IPOs.

... As of press time, according to CoinGecko data: BTC price is $74,678.15, a 24-hour change of +0.6%; ETH price is $2,353.71, a 24-hour change of +1.2%; BNB price is $621.88, a 24-hour change of +1.2%. SOL price is $84.88, up 1.1% in the last 24 hours; DOGE price is $0.09472, up 1.7% in the last 24 hours; XRP price is $1.39, up 1.7% in the last 24 hours; TRX price is $0.327, up 1.0% in the last 24 hours. WLFI price is $0.08054, a 24-hour change of -0.2%; HYPE price is $44.50, a 24-hour change of +2.3%. Policy: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) is investigating a series of crude oil futures trades that occurred "abnormally precisely at the time" before Trump's shift in policy toward Iran. On April 16, sources revealed that the CFTC was investigating a series of crude oil futures trades that occurred before Trump's shift in policy toward Iran. The CFTC is leading an investigation into crude oil futures trading on the CME Group and Intercontinental Exchange platforms and has requested the two exchanges to submit relevant data, including the so-called "Tag 50" identifier used to identify trading entities. On March 23, billions of dollars worth of crude oil and stock index futures were traded 15 minutes before Trump announced a delay to his previously threatened strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure. Trump's subsequent statement caused crude oil prices to plummet and stock markets to surge. A similar situation occurred on April 7 before Trump announced a two-week ceasefire agreement with Iran. Futures trading activity increased hours before the announcement, and the subsequent news triggered a drop in oil and natural gas prices. On April 16, a written statement indicated that Michael Selig, Chairman of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), pledged to Congress to punish fraud and insider trading. The CFTC chairman stated, "We will find you, and you will face full legal sanctions." On April 16th, local time, on April 15th, a majority of US senators supported President Trump's military action against Iran. The Senate voted 52-47 to reject a Democratic-led resolution aimed at preventing war until hostilities were authorized by Congress. (CCTV) On April 16th, according to US sources on the 15th, President Trump stated that the US "very likely" to reach an agreement with Iran before King Charles III's visit to the US in late April. In an interview, Trump stated that the possibility of reaching an agreement was "very high." According to the schedule, King Charles III and Queen Camilla will visit the United States from April 27 to 30. (CCTV News) Iran will assess whether to hold the next round of negotiations with the United States. On April 16, Iran's Tasnim News Agency, citing sources, reported on the 15th that after meeting with a delegation led by Pakistani Army Chief of Staff Munir, Iran will conduct a necessary assessment before deciding whether to hold the next round of negotiations with the United States. The source said that the ceasefire in Lebanon would be a positive signal for Iran's participation in the next round of negotiations. At the same time, the US must adhere to a reasonable negotiation framework and not make excessive demands or violate commitments made before the ceasefire. (Xinhua) Lebanese Officials: Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire Agreement to be Announced "Soon," Duration May Be Linked to US-Iran Truce On April 16, according to the Financial Times, Lebanese officials revealed that a ceasefire agreement between Israel and Lebanon aimed at ending more than six weeks of fighting is expected to be announced "soon" under pressure from the United States to ease tensions in the Middle East. Sources indicated that the ceasefire between Israel and the Lebanese armed group Hezbollah could take effect "within this week," most likely after Israeli ground forces complete their capture of the southern Lebanese city of Bint Jubail. The conflict in Lebanon has become a sticking point in broader efforts to end the US-Israel war against Iraq, which began on February 28. Lebanese officials pointed out that once a ceasefire is announced, its duration is likely to be linked to the duration of the US-Iran ceasefire agreement. One Lebanese official stated that the ceasefire will cover a halt to Israeli attacks but not the withdrawal of Israeli troops. Other officials indicated that the ceasefire effort is led by the United States.

Blockchain Applications

▌US Senator Warren Warren Warren Warren Warren Warren recently expressed concerns about Elon Musk's planned April launch of the payment platform X Money, stating that it could pose risks to consumer protection, national security, and the stability of the financial system. Warren pointed out that after acquiring and reshaping X, Musk is pushing it to become a "super app" covering "the entire financial life of users," potentially even replacing the traditional banking system. She questioned X's performance in security and content governance, arguing that this undermines its credibility in the consumer finance sector. Furthermore, Warren criticized the current regulatory environment for becoming too lenient, including the GENIUS Act promoted by the Trump administration, which provides a "special channel" for private companies to issue stablecoins, potentially weakening necessary regulatory constraints.

US Senator Warren Warren Warren recently expressed concerns about Elon Musk's planned April launch of the payment platform X Money, stating that it could pose risks to consumer protection, national security, and the stability of the financial system.

▌Tether Participates in Stablecoin Development Corporation's $134 Million Funding Round

Stablecoin issuer Tether announced its participation through its investment arm in a $134 million funding round for Stablecoin Development Corporation, a company dedicated to providing public market investment channels for the stablecoin economy and promoting the development of digital asset infrastructure. This round also included participation from institutions such as Framework Ventures. Stablecoin Development Corporation, as an on-chain holding platform, is building an ecosystem around the practical applications of stablecoins and DeFi, covering scenarios such as payments, transfers, and cross-platform fund flows, and is committed to optimizing user experience and lowering the barriers to entry.

▌AI Startup Hilbert Completes $28 Million Series A Funding Round

On April 15th, according to the AXIOS website, artificial intelligence startup Hilbert completed a $28 million Series A funding round led by Andreessen Horowitz.

... This startup aims to help businesses automate growth-driving business decisions, thereby changing the current situation where companies invest heavily in artificial intelligence but may not necessarily see returns. Cryptocurrency ▌Circle's Tokenized Money Market Fund USYC: Approximately $2.55 Billion Issued Concentrated on BNB Chain April 15th news: According to the latest data from on-chain data analysis platforms such as DeFiLlama, Circle's tokenized money market fund USYC has reached a total size of $2.68 billion, becoming the world's largest on-chain money market fund. Of this, approximately 95% of the assets (approximately $2.55 billion) have been migrated and issued on BNB Chain, with only the remaining 5% distributed across other networks such as Ethereum.

▌Morgan Stanley Bitcoin ETF MSBT has purchased $83.6 million worth of BTC this week

On April 15th, on-chain analytics platform Arkham publicly disclosed the on-chain wallet address of the Morgan Stanley Bitcoin ETF ($MSBT) for the first time. Data shows that MSBT has cumulatively purchased $83.6 million worth of Bitcoin since its inception this week, and its on-chain address currently holds $64.4 million.

▌Strive purchased 27 BTC, bringing its total holdings to 13,768 BTC

On April 15th, US-listed Bitcoin treasury company Strive ($ASST) purchased 27 BTC, bringing its total holdings to 13,768 BTC.

**TeraWulf Raises $900 Million Through Stock Issuance to Build AI Data Center** On April 15th, Bitcoin mining company TeraWulf raised $900 million through a stock issuance, with the funds earmarked for building an AI data center. **Kraken Secretly Files for US IPO, Valuation Drops to $13.3 Billion** On April 15th, Arjun Sethi, co-CEO of cryptocurrency exchange Kraken, confirmed that the company has secretly filed for an initial public offering (IPO) with the US Securities and Exchange Commission. This IPO application officially proceeded after its initial disclosure last year. Data shows that Kraken's latest valuation is approximately $13.3 billion, a significant decrease from $20 billion in 2025. Previously, Kraken had suspended its IPO plans due to the sluggish cryptocurrency market. Deutsche Börse Group has committed $200 million in exchange for a 1.5% fully diluted ownership stake in Kraken.

▌Coinbase Adds Diem (DIEM) and Opengradient (OPG) to its Listing Roadmap

On April 16, according to an official announcement, Coinbase announced that it has added Diem (DIEM) and Opengradient (OPG) to its listing roadmap.

Important Economic Developments

▌White House Denies Request for Extension of US-Iran Ceasefire, Emphasizes Pakistan as Sole Mediator

On April 16, White House Press Secretary Caroline Levitt stated on April 15 that the United States has not formally requested an extension of the ceasefire agreement with Iran, and related reports are "untrue." The two sides are currently continuing contact and negotiations.

Levitt stated that the Trump administration considers the negotiations "constructive" and is optimistic about the prospects of reaching an agreement, while emphasizing that all decisions are subject to official White House announcements. She also pointed out that Pakistan played a key mediating role in this round of negotiations, calling it the "only mediator" at present. Despite expressions of willingness to assist from many countries, the US hopes to continue communication and negotiations through Pakistani channels. (CCTV News)

▌Fed Beige Book Shows Economic Activity Remains Growing, Energy Costs Rise Significantly

On April 16, the Federal Reserve stated that economic activity continued to grow at a slight to moderate pace in most districts amid renewed uncertainty from the Iran war and rising energy costs. The Fed's Beige Book, released Wednesday, showed that overall price growth remained moderate, but energy and fuel costs rose "significantly" in all 12 districts.

... The Beige Book states, "The Middle East conflict is seen as a major source of uncertainty, complicating business choices regarding hiring, pricing, and capital investment, with many companies adopting a wait-and-see approach." ▌Sources: US and Iran Considering Two-Week Ceasefire Extension On April 15, a source familiar with the matter revealed that the United States and Iran are considering extending the ceasefire agreement for two weeks to allow more time for peace negotiations. The current temporary ceasefire is set to expire on April 22. The source indicated that mediators between the warring parties are attempting to arrange technical talks to resolve the most contentious issues, including the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and Iran's uranium enrichment activities. The source stated that if successful, these talks could pave the way for the next round of negotiations between senior officials from both countries. A US official familiar with the situation stated that there is still no guarantee that the ceasefire will be extended, and the US has not yet agreed to do so. The Iranian Foreign Ministry did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

▌Israel Sets Three Basic Conditions for Ceasefire Agreement with Lebanon

On April 16, local time on the evening of the 15th, according to Israeli sources, the Israeli military set three basic conditions for a ceasefire agreement with Lebanon: the Israeli military demands the establishment of a buffer zone extending to the Litani River and a ban on Hezbollah activities in the area; the Israeli military demands complete freedom of military action north of the Litani River, aimed at eliminating the threats facing Israel; and the Israeli military demands the disarmament of Hezbollah through long-term steps under US supervision.

... (CCTV News) Iranian Foreign Ministry Spokesperson: Extension of Iran-US Ceasefire Unconfirmed On April 16, Iranian Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Baghae stated at a press conference in Tehran on April 15 that speculation regarding an extension of the Iran-US ceasefire could not be confirmed, and it was necessary to observe whether the US demonstrated genuine sincerity in its stated diplomatic stance. According to the Islamic Republic News Agency (IRNA), Baghae, in response to questions about the possibility of an extension of the Iran-US ceasefire and which issues had made relative progress in the negotiations, stated that the US "has repeatedly changed its position and made contradictory statements," and even if some consensus is reached, it is difficult to guarantee its implementation. Therefore, the dialogue between Iran and the US is proceeding under very complex circumstances. Regarding the progress of the negotiations, Baghae stated that the relevant issues are interconnected, and all aspects need to be advanced as a whole. Currently, the dialogue continues through Pakistan as an intermediary. (Xinhua News Agency) Federal Reserve's Mossaleem: Interest Rates Need to Remain Unchanged for Some Time On April 16, Federal Reserve Chairman Mossaleem stated on Wednesday that high oil prices could push the underlying inflation rate for the remainder of the year nearly one percentage point higher than the Fed's 2% target, and the Fed may need to keep interest rates unchanged. Mossaleem said, "We are likely to see some transmission of oil prices to core inflation," and that by the end of the year, the underlying measure of price increases will be "slightly below 3%, perhaps around 3%," with the risk of further upside. Mossaleem said the Fed is likely to keep its policy rate in the current range of 3.50%-3.75% for "some time," while monitoring inflation, employment, and economic data in the coming months; many of his colleagues share this view. The impact of last year's tariff increases may gradually fade this quarter, and housing price inflation is also weakening. With rising oil prices and persistently high inflation rates across a range of service sectors, he would be open to raising interest rates if inflation begins to rise and potentially drive up inflation expectations. Musalaim also stated that the oil market represents the "third negative supply shock in 12 months," and coupled with rising tariffs and stricter immigration regulations, the inflation outlook and the job market face risks, potentially impacting economic growth. He believes economic growth this year will slow, but will remain between 1.5% and 2%. According to CME's "FedWatch" on April 16th, the probability of the Fed raising interest rates by 25 basis points in April is 1.6%, while the probability of keeping rates unchanged is 98.4%. The probability of the Fed cumulatively cutting rates by 25 basis points by June is 0%, the probability of keeping rates unchanged is 98%, and the probability of cumulatively raising rates by 25 basis points is 2%.

Golden Encyclopedia

▌Can Bitcoin Still Be Called "Digital Gold" During Geopolitical Crises?

Every geopolitical crisis causes gold prices to rise while Bitcoin prices plummet. After six tests, the claim of "digital gold" has never been supported by data. Countries hoard gold but exclude Bitcoin from their reserves. For investors, Bitcoin exhibits asymmetry: it falls with stocks but does not rise with them. Three structural asymmetries prevent Bitcoin from achieving safe-haven status: an oversupply of derivatives (market structure), the dominance of leveraged traders (participant composition), and a lack of repeatable behavioral records (behavioral accumulation).

Bitcoin is not a safe-haven asset, but it is a "crisis-useful asset," which can indeed function when borders are closed and banks fail. If these three asymmetries are reduced, Bitcoin may no longer be a replica of gold but rather a completely new "next-generation gold."

Generational shifts and the widespread application of algorithms are key factors that could accelerate this process.

data.title: Analysis of the operational mechanism, market structure and risks of on-chain vaults
data.description:
Author: Tanay Ved Source: coinmetrics Translation: Shan Ouba, Jinse Finance

Key Points

  • Vaults are non-custodial smart contracts that can collect user deposits and invest them in yield-generating strategies, providing a transparent and composable alternative to traditional funds and structured products.

  • Based on the ERC-4626 Vault standard and modular lending protocols, and with the expansion of stablecoins, tokenized reality assets (RWA), and interest-bearing assets, vaults have grown into a core on-chain infrastructure.

  • Products such as Morpho, Sky's sUSDS, and Ethena's sUSDe demonstrate that vaults can cover diverse scenarios such as savings products, neutral hedging strategies, and selective lending, each with its own unique source of returns and risk characteristics.

  • Vault management is becoming a professional business, with many institutions managing billions of dollars in assets and proactively managing collateral, liquidity, and oracle risks. Vaults have become a core component of the on-chain ecosystem, aggregating funds and investing them in yield-generating strategies such as lending markets, staking protocols, or tokenized real-world assets (RWA). Similar to professionally managed investment funds or structured products, vaults aggregate funds into pre-defined investment portfolios to match different risk appetites, while offering greater transparency, composability, and capital utilization efficiency. As the types of effective on-chain assets continue to expand, from stablecoins and interest-bearing tokens to RWA, vaults naturally become the core layer for fund aggregation. Its adoption rate is accelerating: asset management institutions such as Bitwise are entering the vault management market, Kraken has embedded vault strategies into its "DeFi Earn" product, and protocols such as Morpho, Spark, and Aave are continuously upgrading their underlying infrastructure. At the same time, vaults also involve inherent trade-offs, with risk management and management design becoming key to the field's development. This article will outline on-chain vaults, the ERC-4626 token standard that supports their operation, analyze the roles of protocols, markets, and risk managers, and focus on analyzing the risk dimensions of vault management and their impact on the current vault market landscape. What is a vault? At its core, a vault is a non-custodial smart contract that can pool funds and execute yield generation strategies, automating asset operations that previously required active monitoring and management across protocols. According to the architecture design, the vault can invest assets in the lending market, implement neutral hedging strategies, pledge/re-pledge assets to obtain validator rewards, or configure tokenized instruments to obtain off-chain yields. The diagram below is a simplified illustration of the core participants and fund flows in a typical vault architecture, covering depositors, managers, and the underlying market that generates yields. The core of the architecture is the vault smart contract (ERC-4626), which is responsible for handling deposit receipts, share issuance, and accounting. The management or strategy manager sets the risk parameters and configuration logic of the vault, and the funds are then invested in the underlying market. Thanks to the common technical standards of mainstream vaults within the current ecosystem, depositors can interact with the vault through a single interface, regardless of the complexity of the underlying strategy.

    ERC-4626: Universal Vault Interface Standard

    In 2022, the tokenized vault standard ERC-4626 was officially launched. As an extension of the ERC-20 token standard, it provides a unified interface for deposits, withdrawals, share issuance, and accounting, eliminating the need for customized development. Today, this standard supports the core functions of various vaults, including lending markets, savings products, and interest-bearing tokens.

    After depositing assets (such as USDC and WETH) into an ERC-4626 vault, users receive share certificates representing their proportional ownership of the underlying assets of the vault. Redeeming these shares allows them to withdraw their principal and all generated returns.

    The number of shares obtained depends on the deposit amount and the current exchange rate (the ratio of total vault assets to total circulating shares). When the vault generates income, the asset size increases while the number of shares remains unchanged, the exchange rate rises accordingly, and the underlying assets redeemable for each share gradually increase. A simple example is as follows:

    1. Deposit 1000 USDC into the USDC Treasury

    2. The exchange rate at the time of deposit is 1.0, and the user receives 1000 units

    3. After one year, the annualized return is 4%, and the exchange rate rises to 1.04

    4. 1000 units can be redeemed for 1040 USDC

    Interest-Rating Stablecoins

    Currently, the most widely used vault products are interest-bearing stablecoins. These tokens passively accrue interest through the ERC-4626 tranche mechanism. Sky's savings-type USDS (sUSDS) and Ethena's collateralized USDe (sUSDe) are the largest representatives, with a total supply exceeding $9.4 billion.

    sUSDS is Sky's savings vault. The Spark protocol allocates USDS to RWA instruments, stablecoins such as USDC, and on-chain and OTC lending markets to provide stable savings rates, essentially acting as an on-chain money market fund. Ethena's sUSDe adopts a different model, using crypto assets and gradually increasing RWA for neutral hedging to obtain funding rate revenue.

    Both tokens achieve continuous value appreciation relative to their underlying assets through the aforementioned ERC-4626 exchange rate mechanism. The supply trends of the two tokens reflect strategic differences: sUSDS achieves steady growth by relying on diversified underlying yields such as RWA, while sUSDe is more sensitive to funding rate environments and overall crypto market conditions. Therefore, Ethena is actively expanding its collateral reserves to include overcollateralized institutional lending, highly liquid RWA, and non-crypto futures exposure. Selected Lending Markets Interest-bearing stablecoins embed fixed strategies within the vault itself, while protocols like Morpho completely separate the vault layer from the strategy/management layer. Morpho vaults are ERC-4626 compliant lending vaults, with risk parameters, collateral access rules, and allocation limits set by independent managers such as Gauntlet, Steakhouse Financial, and Bitwise. Depositors earn interest by depositing a single asset (such as USDC), while the managers allocate funds to lending markets such as BTC/USDC and WETH/USDC.

    Data source: Morpho Protocol Docs

    This modular infrastructure is supporting more upper-layer products: Coinbase provides BTC collateralized loans directly on the client side using Morpho, Kraken integrates its vault strategy into its DeFi Earn product, and Bitwise became one of the first traditional asset management institutions to launch a non-custodial USDC vault on Morpho and enter the vault management market.

    Vault Case Comparison Table

    Besides Morpho, a leading project with deposits of $11.5 billion, Spark serves as the core lending and allocation layer of Sky sUSDS, while Aave v4 shifts from a unified liquidity pool to an isolated vault market with modular risk parameters.

    Data Source: CM Network Data Pro

    Collection Risk

    Low-quality or low-liquidity collateral can lead to bad debts. If the price of collateral falls faster than the liquidation execution speed, the vault will have undercollateralized positions. In March 2026, Resolv Labs' USR token collapsed due to an attacker minting 80 million uncollateralized tokens, causing bad debts in multiple vaults on Morpho, Euler, and Fluid. The management controls this risk through a conservative LTV ratio, borrowing limits, and a strict collateral whitelist. Oracle Risk: Price data source failures, manipulation, or hard-coded errors can lead to improper liquidation or allow under-collateralized lending. In October 2025, despite Ethena's intact collateral, USDe's price on Binance fell to $0.65 due to internal oracles referencing order books with insufficient depth. Oracle source selection and backup plans are central to the management's design, typically employing a multi-source oracle configuration to avoid referencing price data from illiquid or single platforms. Data Source: CM Market Data Feed

    Data Source: CM Market Data Feed

    Conclusion

    Vaults are rapidly becoming the core configuration and abstraction layer for on-chain asset management. Relying on ERC-4626 standardization, institutional-grade selection strategies, and modular lending infrastructure, vaults have become a primary means of obtaining stablecoin yields, RWA exposure, and more complex basis and credit transactions. In the future, vaults will become the underlying support for on-chain credit and fixed-income products, transforming more complex off-chain exposures into programmable and interoperable basic components.

    ... The scaling up of this field requires a balance between risk exposure and returns, especially given the increasing number of off-chain assets and complex strategies being implemented on-chain.

data.title: Golden Web3.0 Daily Report | Polygon Launches Liquidity Staking Token sPOL
data.description:
DeFi data

1. Total market value of DeFi tokens: US$53.65 billion

DeFi Total Market Cap Data Source: coingecko

2. Trading volume of decentralized exchanges in the past 24 hours: $40.09

title="7453279" alt="wxgQtDyM82ZgGslifuOOCH1Uod4KVXFC6tSD867K.png">

Trading volume of decentralized exchanges in the past 24 hours. Data source: coingecko

3. Assets locked in DeFi: $973.06 billion$

Top 10 DeFi Projects by Locked Assets and Total Value Locked (Data Source: defillama)

NFT Data

1. Total Market Value of NFTs: $7.651 Billion

alt="bQx7kU5uFnhKi0E3pmd2IJQg5FnZrktg8inwxA75.png">

NFT Total Market Capitalization, Top Ten Projects by Market Capitalization Data Source: Coinmarketcap

2.24-hour NFT transaction volume: $1.811 billionUSD

    AI Hot Topics

    1. US President Trump: The Anthropic Threat is Not Systemic

    On April 15th, US President Trump stated: The Anthropic threat is not systemic. (Regarding the Anthropic issue) We will resolve it.

    2. Latest Harvard Research: 80% of Initial Diagnoses Using AI Large Language Models are Wrong

    On April 15th, a research team at Harvard Medical School evaluated the diagnostic capabilities of over 20 state-of-the-art AI Large Language Models (LLMs), including ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude. The results showed that the error rate for "differential diagnosis" (identifying possible diseases) based on a patient's initial symptoms and signs was as high as 80%. This study further showed that with more test results provided by the patient, the large model could reduce the failure rate of the "final diagnosis" to around 40%. Researchers stated that this means AI chatbots need sufficient patient information to make more accurate diagnoses; when patients cannot provide complete health test information, the results provided by AI are unreliable.

    3. Anthropic Introduces Identity Verification Mechanism in Certain Claude Use Cases

    According to an official website announcement, Anthropic has introduced an identity verification mechanism in certain Claude use cases to prevent abuse, enforce usage policies, and fulfill legal obligations. This process is powered by Persona, and users are required to submit a government-issued photo ID and may need to perform real-time selfie verification. Anthropic states that the verification data is used only to confirm identity and is not used for model training, marketing, or advertising. If verification fails, users can retry multiple times within the process or submit a form for manual assistance. Accounts may be banned for repeated violations of usage policies, terms of service, registration from unsupported regions, or use by users under the age of 18. Previous news: Anthropic received investment offers from multiple venture capital firms, with a valuation as high as $800 billion.

    ...

    4. French AI and Digital Affairs Official: Tokenization Has Moved from Vision to Reality; France Will Support Innovation with a Balanced Approach

    On April 15th, at the 2026 Paris Blockchain Week summit, @alehenanff, an official from the French Ministry of Artificial Intelligence and Digital Affairs, delivered a keynote speech, officially clarifying France's core development stance on the blockchain and digital asset industry. In his speech, @alehenanff made a significant official statement regarding the tokenization trend: "Tokenization is no longer a promise. It’s reality." 5. JPMorgan Chase CEO: AI is Not Just a Tool, It's a Threat On April 15th, Jamie Dimon, CEO of JPMorgan Chase (JPM.N), stated on Tuesday that while artificial intelligence tools may ultimately help businesses defend against cyberattacks, at this stage, they actually make businesses more vulnerable. “Artificial intelligence makes things worse and more difficult,” Dimon told analysts on Tuesday morning’s earnings call. “It does introduce additional vulnerabilities, and perhaps in the long run, it also provides better ways to strengthen ourselves.” These remarks indicate that artificial intelligence, a technology that businesses see as a productivity booster, has also evolved into a serious threat because it provides malicious actors with new ways to infiltrate technological systems. Last week, U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessant and Federal Reserve Chairman Powell convened a meeting with bank CEOs to discuss the risks posed by Mythos, a cutting-edge AI model designed by Anthropic specifically for defensive cybersecurity workflows.

    DeFi Hot Topics

    1. eToro to Acquire Zengo Protocol for $70 Million to Expand Self-Custed Crypto Capabilities

    On April 15th, trading and investment platform eToro announced an agreement to acquire the self-custodied crypto wallet Zengo for $70 million. This acquisition aims to expand its self-custodied crypto capabilities and strengthen its strategy of connecting traditional finance with on-chain infrastructure. Upon completion of the transaction, eToro will integrate its multi-asset platform with Zengo's non-custodial wallet technology to support the development of digital asset use cases such as tokenized assets, prediction markets, and perpetual contracts.

    2. Polkadot Hyperbridge Attackers Transfer All $269,000 Stolen Funds to Tornado Cash

    According to real-time alerts from on-chain analytics platform Arkham, the hacker address that previously attacked the Polkadot Hyperbridge has transferred all the stolen funds of $269,000 (approximately $269,000, settled in ETH) to the privacy protocol Tornado Cash for money laundering through mixing.

    3. LI.FI Launches On-Chain Yield Product LI.FIEarn for Institutions

    On April 15, the cross-chain protocol LI.FI announced the launch of a new product, LI.FIEarn, which provides on-chain yield functionality for enterprises with digital asset strategies. It supports strategies that can be integrated into more than 20 vault protocols through a single integration and has built-in cross-chain execution capabilities across more than 60 chains.

    3.LI.FI Launches On-Chain Yield Product LI.FIEarn for Institutions

    4. Ethereum On-Chain Social Protocol EFP Completes Integration with Etherscan, Supporting On-Chain Social Data Display

    On April 15th, brantly.eth, a core contributor to the Ethereum On-Chain Social Protocol EFP (EFP), announced the completion of EFP's integration with the blockchain explorer Etherscan. Users can now view an account's ENS domain, avatar, and on-chain EFP followers and the number of followers in the "Cards" tab on each Etherscan account page.

    4. Ethereum On-Chain Social Protocol EFP Completes Integration with Etherscan, Supporting On-Chain Social Data Display

    5.Lattice Announces Phase-Out Shutdown; Layer 2 Network Redstone to Cease Operations on May 16

    On April 15th, Lattice, a blockchain game infrastructure team, announced that it will gradually shut down its business after five years of operation. Its Layer 2 network, Redstone, will cease operation on May 16th, 2026 at 07:59. Lattice reminded users to withdraw funds before this deadline, especially assets in contracts such as Uniswap pools. After the shutdown, only L1 withdrawal contracts will be deployed for external account balance withdrawals; funds in contracts will be irrecoverable. It is understood that Lattice was founded in 2021 and developed tools such as the MUD framework, the Redstone chain, Quarry, and Dozer, but failed to achieve a sustainable business model. The team used remaining funds for the final project, DUST Autonomous World, but the scale was insufficient to support the business, and they did not believe that VC financing was the right path.

    ... Disclaimer: Jinse Finance, as a blockchain information platform, publishes articles for informational purposes only and should not be considered actual investment advice. Please establish sound investment principles and be sure to enhance your risk awareness.

data.title: How to build a new operating system for AI Agent?
data.description:
Written by: imToken

2026 is becoming a crucial turning point for the deep convergence of Crypto and AI.

Over the past two years, we have witnessed AI's transformation from an "auxiliary tool" to an "autonomous economic participant." AI agents are no longer just chatbots answering questions; they are beginning to autonomously initiate transactions, call APIs, manage asset portfolios, and even hire other agents to complete tasks.

But all of this is predicated on these agents needing identity, payment channels, reputation records, and a verifiable execution environment.

And these are precisely the problems that blockchain excels at solving.

These are precisely the problems that blockchain excels at solving.

... As we've discussed before, the Ethereum Foundation established the Decentralized AI (dAI) team in September 2025, and Vitalik Buterin published a systematic AI strategic framework in early 2026. A series of protocol standards surrounding agent identity, payment, and execution are already running on the mainnet. Meanwhile, new public chain ecosystems like Solana are building AI infrastructure along their own paths. Therefore, this article attempts to outline the complete landscape of current crypto AI protocols, focusing on the Ethereum ecosystem and supplementing it with important developments from other public chains. I. Vitalik's AI Blueprint: Ethereum to be the "Trust Layer" of the AI ​​World In February 2026, Vitalik Buterin published a systematic post on X, specifically revising his "Crypto × AI" cross-framework proposed two years prior. In his article, he re-examined the ideas he proposed two years ago, arguing that the accelerated push towards general artificial intelligence often resembles the unrestricted speed and scale challenged when Ethereum was created. He explicitly opposes simplifying AI development into an "AGI race," instead advocating that Ethereum should be a directional guide for the AI ​​world. In other words, his real concern is not how to make AI spiral out of control faster, but how to ensure that AI expansion is built on verifiable, auditable, and constrained infrastructure. Overall, Vitalik's framework comprises four core pillars. First is trustworthy AI interaction tools. He advocates using tools such as local large language models (Local LLMs) and zero-knowledge proof payment mechanisms to allow users to use AI services without exposing their identity and raw data. This attitude goes beyond mere abstract expression. In April 2026, Vitalik publicly shared his local LLM usage solution. After testing multiple hardware setups, he chose to run the 35 billion-parameter open-source model Qwen3.5 locally on a computer equipped with an NVIDIA 5090 GPU, performing all calculations locally. His goal was to improve inference speed to a level usable in daily use and minimize reliance on cloud models. Of course, the symbolic significance of this event outweighs its practical significance, but it demonstrates that, at least in his view, the truly worthwhile direction for AI is not just stronger models, but more controllable models. Secondly, there's the economic coordination layer of AI. This includes Ethereum's ability to support inter-agent payments, security deposits, dispute resolution, and reputation building through smart contracts, enabling programmable economic relationships between machines. Thirdly, there's AI as the interface for Web3; for example, local AI assistants can help users draft transactions, audit smart contracts, and interpret formal verification proofs, becoming a bridge for ordinary people to enter the complex on-chain world. Finally, there's the AI-enhanced governance system, such as using AI to upgrade prediction markets, quadratic voting, and public fund allocation mechanisms, finding a balance between automation and human judgment. In summary, the core concept of this framework can be summarized in one sentence: Ethereum isn't about accelerating AI, but about enabling AI to operate in a verifiable, auditable, and decentralized environment. So how exactly will this be achieved? II. From Identity Protocols to Payment Protocols, to Execution Protocols, and to Verifiable AI If Vitalik's framework is the macro blueprint, then the recent wave of protocol evolution in the Ethereum ecosystem has begun to push this methodology into specific technology stacks. The first infrastructure node most noteworthy is ERC-8004. As Ethereum's standard for identity, reputation, and verification designed for AI Agents, it was spearheaded by the Ethereum Foundation's dAI team, in collaboration with Google, Coinbase, and MetaMask, and encompasses almost all three key entry points: AI, transactions, and wallets. As its official name suggests, Trustless Agents, its core logic isn't about complex algorithms, but rather about giving AI verifiable identities, reputations, and proofs of ability on the blockchain.
